post.summary: Send an OTP code to phone or email for register/login
post.description: Generates a 6-digit OTP and sends it to exactly one target: phone (via 019sms) or email (via SMTP). In fixed mode (AUTH_FIXED_OTP_ENABLED=true), OTP delivery is bypassed and AUTH_FIXED_OTP_CODE is accepted for verification.

post.summary: Verify an OTP code previously sent to phone or email
post.description: Marks the (phone/email, purpose) pair as OTP-verified for a short window. The verified OTP is then consumed when calling /auth/register or /auth/login with the same identifier and purpose.

post.summary: Register a new account or extend user role (requires OTP-verified phone)
post.description: Creates a new user with email and phone, or extends an existing user with the supplied role. The phone must have been OTP-verified via /auth/request-otp + /auth/verify-otp with purpose=register prior to this call.

post.summary: Login with OTP (email OR phone)
post.description: Authenticates a user using an OTP code. Provide either email or phone (exactly one) along with the OTP code. This endpoint verifies and consumes the OTP internally, so you do not need to call /auth/verify-otp as a separate step.

post.summary: Rotate refresh token and issue a new token pair
Logout current user by revoking refresh token
Issue anonymous token for unauthenticated flows
Link anonymous session data to authenticated user
